references.text: Amoroso, VB, PJF Orias, JC Nobleza, AJD Lagumbay, IA Adlaon, MCS Salolog & FP Coritico. 2025. New distribution record of Matonia foxworthyi Copel. from Mt. Redondo, Dinagat Islands, the Philippines, with insights on its anatomy and ecology. Philippine Journal of Science 154: 379-385.
Barcelona, JF & PB Pelser. 2014. Phanerosorus (Matoniaceae), a new fern genus record for the Philippines. Phytotaxa 170: 133-135.
Kato, M. 1993. A taxonomic study of the genus Matonia (Matoniaceae). Blumea 38: 167-172
Kato, M. 1998. Matoniaceae. Flora Malesiana series 2, 3: 289-294

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 7 May 2025

species.citation: Copel. , PJS 3c (1908) 343 ; Kato, Fl. Males. ser. 2, 3 (1998) 292; Amoroso et al., PJS 154 (2025) 379.
species.distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines. DINAGAT, MINDORO, SIBUYAN.
species.notes: P.B. Pelser (7-May-25): contrary to Amoroso et al. (2025), Matonia foxworthyi was first reported from Dinagat in Co's Digital Flora of the Philippines in 2014 based on photos taken by Pelser & Barcelona. (420-)750-2100m.

species.citation: R.Br. , Pl. Asiat. Rar. 1 (1829) 16 ; Kato, Fl. Males. ser. 2, 3 (1998) 291.
species.distribution: Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand. MINDORO.
species.notes: J.F. Barcelona (18-Sep-23): Known from the Philippines from Stone et al. PPI 755 (US). (90-)750-2000m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Julie F. Barcelona, 13 July 2019

species.citation: Diels , Notizbl. Bot. Gart. Berlin-Dahlem 11 (1932) 311 ; Kato, Fl. Males. ser. 2, 3 (1998) 294.
